setting.note: Through three sisters of the village of al-Awafi in the interior of Oman, Alharthi traces a country moving in a single lifetime from a slave-owning, date-farming past into oil modernity. The world of the book is the interior: the village and its date plantations and irrigation channels, the desert around it, and the merchant households; beyond it lie the capital Muscat, where the new Oman is made, and Zanzibar, from which the enslaved were once carried across the sea.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Jokha Alharthi, first published 2010 (Sayyidat al-Qamar). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Celestial Bodies, Jokha Alharthi (2010). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The gold of the desert sun and the dates, and of the oil wealth about to remake Oman in a single generation. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
